differentials.name: Pheochromocytoma ◊  Massive bleed ← an undiagnosed pheochromocytoma has been lethal in 50%!
differentials.raw: Pheochromocytoma ◊  Massive bleed ← an undiagnosed pheochromocytoma has been lethal in 50%!

differentials.name: flank pain of sudden onset ◊  Follow-up CT may be indicated at 3 and 6 months if the source of blood remains indeterminate!
differentials.raw: flank pain of sudden onset ◊  Follow-up CT may be indicated at 3 and 6 months if the source of blood remains indeterminate!

context: ◊  Surgical exploration must be considered to uncover a small renal tumor if the cause of hemorrhage is not determined radiologically! Subcapsular Hematoma √  subcapsular mass with flattening of renal parenchyma √  total resorption / formation of pseudocapsule with calcification Angio: √  avascular mass Cx:  Page kidney (ischemia, release of renin, HTN)
